data.title: Samsung Electronics Agrees on 5.1% Wage Increase for 2025
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Samsung Electronics' management and union negotiators have agreed on a 5.1% wage increase for this year.

Each Samsung employee will also receive 30 stocks and a two-million-point online shopping reward, which can be used to purchase Samsung goods, as perks under the preliminary deal, the South Korean technology company said in a statement Monday.

Members of the National Samsung Electronics Union, which currently claims to have around 36,000 members, will vote on the deal for approval by March 5, the company said.

Management and union representatives will also launch a joint task force to enhance the bonus system, it added.

Unionized Samsung workers last year launched their first-ever walkout to press for higher wages and other demands. The labor unrest, however, caused no major disruptions to highly automated chip-making productions.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: New German Government Should Focus on Economic Turnaround and Innovation, Industry Group Says — Market Talk
data.description: Germany's incoming administration must drive an economic turnaround and foster innovation to restore the country's competitiveness, the Federation of German Industries (BDI) says in a statement. Policymakers should prioritize economic growth to counteract declining industrial production and Germany's weakening position as a business hub, the industry group says. The BDI outlines four key priorities for the new government to focus on: enhancing competitiveness, promoting innovation, modernizing the state, and strengthening Germany's global standing. The group proposes lowering energy prices--which have risen since the start of Russia's invasion of Ukraine-- lower company taxes and a reduction to bureaucracy, among others.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Shares Close Mixed; Property Stocks Gain — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares closed mixed, with the Shanghai Composite Index dropping 0.2% to 3373.03. The Shenzhen Composite edged 0.1% higher, while the ChiNext Price Index was 0.7% lower. Investors are awaiting the upcoming Two Sessions meetings for more policy signals from Beijing. Among major stocks, property stocks advanced. Poly Developments & Holdings was 1.8% higher and China Merchants Shekou Industrial Zone Holdings rose 3.4%. Decliners included Hygon Information Technology, which was 1.85% lower, and BYD, down 1.75%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore Airlines Fair Value Estimate Raised to S$6.50 From S$6.30 by OCBC Investment Research >C6L.SG
Ningbo Energy Group Establishes New Subsidiary for Solar Power Project
data.description:
Ningbo Energy Group subsidiary Langchen New Energy invested in the establishment of wholly owned Ma'anshan Langchen.

Ma'anshan Langchen, which has a registered capital of 5 million yuan, aims to develop photovoltaic power generation projects as part of Ningbo Energy's strategy to expand its renewable energy sector and accelerate its green energy transition, according to a Feb. 22 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the steam and electricity generation company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Lenovo's Revenue Should Get Boost From Premium PC Offerings — Market Talk
data.description: Lenovo's PC volumes should stabilize in calendar 2025, supported by an accelerated Windows 11 replacement cycle, CGS International analyst Ray Kwok says in a note. He projects Lenovo's PC revenue to rise 1% and 3% for FY 2025 and FY 2026, respectively, as average selling prices rise due to better product mix and bigger contributions by premium PCs, such as gaming PCs and AI PCs. CGS Interational raises its FY 2026 and FY 2027 EPS forecasts on Lenovo by 13%-15% as the global PC market recovery drives demand for AI PCs and servers, supporting profitability. The brokerage raises the stock's target to HK$15.00 from HK$13.30 and maintains an add rating. Shares are last at HK$13.02. (hoishan.chan@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore to Launch SG$5 Billion Program to Revitalize Stock Market
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has outlined plans to launch a SG$5 billion program to help boost the competitiveness of the city-state's equities market, according to a media release on Friday.

Through the program, the financial regulator will invest with selected fund managers capable of executing investment mandates focused on Singapore stocks.

"These strategies should be actively managed, invest in a range of companies and not just index component stocks, and over time draw in investments from other investors," MAS said in the release.

The Equity Market Development Program will be funded by MAS' investment portfolio and the financial sector development fund.

data.title: Turkish Business Morale Highest in 8 Months
data.description:
The manufacturing confidence index in Turkey rose to 102.4 in February 2025, up from 100.9 in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since last June.

data.title: Austal Fiscal H1 Results Solid, Poised for 'Very Bright' Medium, Long term, Euroz Harlteys Says
data.description:
Austal fiscal first half results were solid, supported by an unexpected rise in revenue and earnings before interest and tax, positioning the company for a "very bright" medium and long-term outlook, according to a February 21 note by Euroz Hartley.

The company reported fiscal first-half earnings of AU$ 0.069 per diluted share, up from AU$ 0.033 per diluted share a year earlier. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected AU$0.066.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$825.7 million, up from AU$717.7 million a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$883.8 million.

Euroz noted that Austal's orderbook has grown to AU$14.2 billion, including options, and expects that a recent commercial ferry win will boost volume in its Asian shipyards, while progress on the strategic ship-building agreement adds further growth potential.

Despite about $750 million remaining in expenditure, Austal has already secured half of the required funding through the submarine module contract, with most of the cash already received, Euroz added.

Additionally, the company has a letter of support from the Australian Government for up to 50% of $300 million for its Final Assembly 2 project, leaving only $150 million to fund, the investment firm said.

Euroz believes that the total sum of opportunities supports Austal becoming an over $3 billion revenue business over the next decade once fully ramped up.

The results have boosted investor confidence in Austal's ability to fund and execute its plans, driving an upward movement in its share price.

Euroz maintained the firm's buy ratings and its price target of AU$4.01.

data.title: Gohigh Networks Ends Partnership With Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm
data.description:
Gohigh Networks ended its partnership with Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm, according to a Feb. 21 disclosure with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Zhongshen Asia Pacific Accounting Firm was previously re-hired to do the 2024 audit, but no agreement was signed as the accounting firm would not be able to finish the audit in time due to its personnel and time arrangement, the Chinese communications equipment manufacturer said.

The company said it will appoint a new accounting firm as soon as possible.

The company's shares closed 5% lower.

data.title: Kosmos Energy Reports Lower FY24 Net Income, Total Revenue
data.description:
Kosmos Energy said Monday its net income and total revenue declined year over year in 2024.

Net income for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $189.9 million, compared with $213.5 million earlier. EPS moved to $0.40 from $0.44.

The upstream oil company's total revenue and other income was $1.68 billion, compared with $1.70 billion a year ago.

data.title: Sumou Real Estate Wins New Infrastructure Development Project in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Sumou Real Estate Co. (SASE:4323) was awarded an infrastructure development project for Sharia Grant Scheme No. 17 from Holy Makkah Municipality in Saudi Arabia.

The real estate company will receive 7.04% of the project's sales after its development, according to a Monday filing.

Sumou said it is following up with the municipality for approval and the signing of the contract.

data.title: Just Eat Takeaway Pretax Loss Narrows, Revenue Misses Market Expectations
data.description:
(06:09 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Adjusted Ebitda EUR460M
(06:10 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Rev EUR5.085B
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Revenue Less Adjusted Order-Fulfilment Costs EUR2.42B
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Total Orders 879M
(06:11 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Total Gross Transaction Value EUR26.3B
(06:13 GMT) *Correct: Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Rev EUR3.56B
(06:13 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Net Loss EUR1.64B
(06:14 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Oper Loss EUR520M
(06:14 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com 2024 Pretax Loss EUR532M
(06:15 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Sees 2025 Constant Currency Gross Transaction Value Growth at 4%-8%
(06:16 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Sees 2025 Adjusted Ebitda at EUR360M-EUR380M
(06:17 GMT) *Just Eat Takeaway.com Targets Adjusted Ebitda Margin of 5% of Gross Transaction Value
(07:21 GMT) Just Eat Takeaway Pretax Loss Narrows, Revenue Misses Market Expectations

By Najat Kantouar

Just Eat Takeaway.com reported a narrowed pretax loss and slightly higher revenue that missed market expectations.

The Amsterdam-headquartered food-delivery group posted a pretax loss for last year of 1.645 billion euros, equivalent to $1.72 billion, compared with a loss of 1.85 billion euros a year earlier. This was driven mainly driven by noncash impairment losses.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose to 460 million euros from 339 million euros, due to an improvement in fulfillment costs per order and efficiencies in marketing, it said.

Revenue increased slightly to 3.56 billion euros from 3.53 billion euros, though was below analyst consensus of 5.16 billion euros taken from FactSet.

Gross transaction value--a key metric for the industry, representing the value of merchandise sold over a certain period--for the year declined 2% in constant currency to 26.3 billion euros. North America GTV in constant currency fell 9%, while Northern Europe grew 4%. U.K. and Ireland grew 4%. The Southern Europe, Australia and New Zealand segment declined 11%.

The company processed 879 million orders last year, down from 926 million a year prior.

For 2025, Just Eat expects constant currency GTV growth--excluding the rest of the world segment--in the range of 4% to 8% on year, with adjusted Ebitda between 360 million and 380 million euros. It also anticipates free cash flow of approximately 100 million euros.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: GSK Closes Up to $1 Billion Purchase of US-based IDRx
data.description:
GSK completed the acquisition of US-based clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company IDRx for a total cash consideration of up to $1.15 billion.

The target company is focused on developing precision therapeutics to treat gastrointestinal stromal tumors.

The pharmaceutical giant made an upfront payment of $1 billion, while the remaining amount is conditional on the success of regulatory approval. GSK added that it will also be responsible for success-based milestone payments and tiered royalties for IDRx's lead molecule IDRX-42 owed to Merck KGaA .

data.title: Market Chatter: Henderson Land Projects See High Demand
data.description:
Henderson Land Development's phase 2 of the Belgravia Place project in Cheung Sha Wan, Hong Kong received 350 orders for its 50 units, making the project oversubscribed seven times, The Standard reported Sunday.

The developer launched the price list with an average discounted price of HK$15,968 per square foot, up 0.4% than the HK$15,902 per square foot in phase 1, the report said.

Henderson also received 800 orders for 63 units at its Eight Southpark project in Kowloon City, Hong Kong, making the project 13 times oversubscribed, the report said.

The units were priced at an average of HK$17,388 per square foot, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch Feb 24
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Monday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 1 hour):

NATIONAL SECURITY COUNCIL

The National Security Council will meet at 1230 GMT to discuss the situation in Ukraine.

PRESIDENT DUDA

U.S. presence in Poland and Central Europe should be boosted, Polish President Andrzej Duda told U.S. President Donald Trump during a short meeting in Washington on Saturday.

INTEREST RATES

If rate cuts in Poland will happen at all in 2025, then they will be at the end of the year and the scale of monetary policy easing would be minor, rate-setter Iwona Duda said in an interview for PAP.

RETAIL SALES

Statistics office will release Polish retail sales data for January at 0900. Analysts polled by Reuters estimate that sales rose 1.5% year-on-year.

UKRAINIAN COMPANIES IN POLAND

In 2022-2024, almost 78,000 companies run by Ukrainians were registered in Poland. Last year was a record, when 33,200 were established, according to a report by the Polish Economic Institute published by Rzeczpospolita daily.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: India's Glenmark Pharma rises on settling anti-trust lawsuit with plaintiffs
data.description:
** Shares of Glenmark Pharmaceuticals up ~4% to 1350.5 rupees

** Co settles remaining antitrust and consumer protection lawsuits with three plaintiffs in connection with generic cholesterol drug Zetia

** Plaintiffs include Humana , Centene Corp

and Kaiser Foundation Health Plan [RIC:RIC:KISERF.UL]

** Under terms of $7 mln settlement, Glenmark denies allegations by plaintiffs, concedes no liability

** Including session's gains, GLEN shares up 48.3% in the last 12 months

data.title: National Grid to Sell U.S. Renewables Unit for $1.7 Billion
data.description:
(07:02 GMT) *National Grid Sells Renewables US Business to Brookfield Asset Management
(07:03 GMT) *National Grid: Deal Implies Enterprise Value for Unit of $1.735B
(07:03 GMT) *National Grid: Final Cash Consideration To Be Subject to Customary Adjustments
(07:04 GMT) *National Grid: Deal to Close in 1H of Fiscal 2026
(07:12 GMT) National Grid to Sell U.S. Renewables Unit for $1.7 Billion

By Elena Vardon

National Grid struck a deal to sell its onshore renewable business in the U.S. to Brookfield Asset Management and its institutional partners for $1.735 billion.

The London-listed utility said Monday that the cash deal was set to close in the first half of the fiscal year ending on March 31, 2026, subject to approvals.

"This transaction is another important step in delivering National Grid's previously communicated strategy to focus on networks and streamline our business," the group said.

National Grid Renewables operates utility-scale, solar, onshore wind and battery storage assets in the U.S.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Germany's Corporate Sector Should See Conservative-Led Government as Positive — Market Talk
data.description: A two-party coalition between the conservative party CDU/CSU and Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Social Democratic Party is now the most likely outcome in Germany's federal election, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. Germany's corporate sector is expected to see a government led by a strong CDU/CSU as positive, they add. It should promise less policy gridlock and uncertainty than under the outgoing government, the analysts write. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: China May Allow Some Flexibility in 2025 Growth Target, Nomura Says — Market Talk
data.description: China may set the 2025 GDP growth target at 4.5%-5% and allow for some flexibility given external uncertainties, Nomura economists say in a research note. Domestic consumption could be a key focus for Beijing this year to counter external headwinds. Authorities are likely to expand existing trade-in programs and other subsidies, the economists say. China could raise its official fiscal deficit to 4% of GDP from 3% in 2024 and may announce record-high issuance of special government bonds to support investment and consumption, they say. Meanwhile, structural reform could accelerate this year, as Beijing places more emphasis on high-quality growth and support for the private sector, they say. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore's Equity Measures Need More Clarity on Focus Sectors — Market Talk
data.description: There needs to be more clarity regarding the focus sectors of Singapore's S$5 billion Equity Market Development Program, says Shekhar Jaiswal, head of equity research at RHB Singapore, in a note. This would ensure the program aligns with sectors that make up the largest portion of the potential initial public offering pipeline for the Singapore Exchange. Jaiswal also stresses the need for greater transparency on how much of the funds will be allocated to small- and mid-cap companies. "Without this clarity, there remains a risk that much of the funds may get allocated to large-cap/or index stocks," he adds.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0713 GMT - There needs to be more clarity regarding the focus sectors of Singapore's S$5 billion Equity Market Development Program, says Shekhar Jaiswal, head of equity research at RHB Singapore, in a note. This would ensure the program aligns with sectors that make up the largest portion of the potential initial public offering pipeline for the Singapore Exchange. Jaiswal also stresses the need for greater transparency on how much of the funds will be allocated to small- and mid-cap companies. "Without this clarity, there remains a risk that much of the funds may get allocated to large-cap/or index stocks," he adds.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - A two-party coalition between the conservative party CDU/CSU and Chancellor Olaf Scholz's Social Democratic Party is now the most likely outcome in Germany's federal election, analysts at Deutsche Bank write. Germany's corporate sector is expected to see a government led by a strong CDU/CSU as positive, they add. It should promise less policy gridlock and uncertainty than under the outgoing government, the analysts write. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0703 GMT - Federal Bank's key priorities over the medium-term may better monetize its balance-sheet strengths, based its analysts' meeting, HDFC Securities analysts say in a research report. The bank highlighted priorities including improved mobilization of current and savings accounts reflecting in lower cost of funds and scaling its medium-yield businesses to drive stronger risk-adjusted returns, the analysts note. The Indian bank has a strategic focus on profitability, but it is likely to undergo an extended investment phase which could push operating-leverage benefits more toward FY 2027. The brokerage trims the stock's target price to INR210.00 from INR220.00, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 1.5% higher at INR182.40. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - StarHub's earnings recovery may be delayed, according to CGS International analysts in a research report. They highlight factors such as intense mobile competition, which is affecting StarHub's average-revenue-per-user growth, and the slower realization of the benefits from its 'DARE+' strategy. Management notes that Singapore's mobile market remains "hypercompetitive" and plans to compete aggressively in 2025 to defend and grow market share. The analysts believe the telecom conglomerate's 2025 earnings outlook is uncertain. The brokerage lowers its 2025-2026 core EPS estimates for StarHub by 11%-17%. CGS International downgrades the stock to hold from add and lowers the stock's target price to S$1.30 from S$1.40. Shares are down 0.8% at S$1.23. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - Singapore's new set of measures to boost the equities market demonstrate a strong governmental commitment to revitalize the domestic equities ecosystem, says Thilan Wickramasinghe, Maybank Securities head of research, in a note. "Of course, this is no silver bullet especially in the context of rising competition from regional exchanges and the revival of Hong Kong/China," he writes. However, the measures could contribute to boosting liquidity and valuations, as well as broadening market participation, while supporting investor safeguards, he says. Maybank expects Singapore Exchange to benefit from higher trading and listing fees. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0602 GMT - DLF is likely to benefit from planned property launches of over INR1 trillion, Ambit Capital analysts say in a research report. The real-estate developer should deliver cumulated pre-sales of INR764 billion over FY 2025-2027, driven by these launches, the analysts estimate. The Indian company's re-entry into Mumbai includes a 2.3-million square feet project with revenue potential of INR58 billion, the analysts note. Success in this project could facilitate DLF's expansion in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, India's largest market by value, the analysts add. The brokerage initiates coverage of the stock with a buy rating and a target price of INR900.00. Shares are 1.4% lower at INR676.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0512 GMT - Ratchthani Leasing appears set to enter an asset-quality recovery cycle, according to Thanachart Securities' Sarachada Sornsong in a research report, as the brokerage reaffirms its buy rating on the stock. The Thai company has taken a more cautious approach to new lending and strengthened its debt collection processes since 2Q 2023, bringing its balance sheet clean-up to near completion, the analyst notes. The financial services provider is expected to maintain its prudent stance, prioritizing loan quality over aggressive portfolio expansion. However, the brokerage lowers its 2025-2027 earnings estimates for Ratchthani Leasing by average 52%, citing factors such as elevated provisions. The target price is reduced to THB1.84 from THB2.64. Shares are 1.2% higher at THB1.66. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Standard Chartered to Spend $1.5 Billion for Wealth Management
data.description:
Standard Chartered will invest $1.5 billion in the bank's wealth management business over the next five years, the South China Morning Post reported Friday, citing Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters.

Activity from Chinese and Indian wealth management clients prompted StanChart to boost its wealth management product, the report said, citing Winters.

The plan comes as Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters said the city will be one of the focus areas for the investment, alongside Singapore, Dubai, and Jersey in the UK, the report said.

"Hong Kong has obviously had a little bit of a tough time over the past few years, but we feel that is stabilizing and actually improving. We are investing in that market as much as we ever have," the report said, citing Winters.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Inox Wind shares down 2% despite becoming first Indian firm to get cryogenic fuel tank certification
data.description:
Shares of Inox India fell 2 percent to Rs 897 in the afternoon on February 24 despite the company becaming India’s first cryogenic equipment manufacturer to receive the prestigious IATF 16949 certification for cryogenic fuel tank manufacturing.

The certification, issued by Bureau Veritas Certification covers the design and manufacturing of insulated cryogenic fuel tanks. The IATF 16949 certification, a mandatory requirement for automotive OEM suppliers, validates a company's quality management standards in the automotive industry.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"There is an evident intent across the globe towards shifting to greener transport fuels, and we firmly believe that our fuel tank solutions, especially for LNG, are capable of servicing this complex yet burgeoning demand," the management said in an exchange filing on February 4.

The certification was awarded following a comprehensive audit of INOX India's facility located in Kalol, in Panchmahal, Gujarat. The certification’s scope encompasses the complete spectrum of design and manufacturing processes with no exclusions, underlining the Company's comprehensive capabilities in this specialized field.

Also read: Black Monday: Sensex plunges 800 pts, Nifty below 22,600; US growth worries among key factors behind market crashThe IATF 16949, is a leading international automotive quality management standard. Being a

requirement for supplies to the automotive industry, IATF 16949 is a global technical specification based on ISO 9001 specific to the automotive industry.

At about 12:30 pm, shares of the company were trading at Rs 906, lower by 1 percent from the last close on the NSE. Inox India shares haven't had a good start to the year, having crashed over 18 percent year-to-date.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Prosus to Acquire Just Eat Takeaway.com in EUR4.1 Billion Deal
data.description:
Prosus (, PRX.JO) and Just Eat Takeaway.com entered into a 4.1 billion-euro merger to create the world's fourth-largest food delivery group, according to separate Monday releases.

Under the deal, the technology investment company is offering to buy the online food delivery company's entire issued share capital for 20.30 euros per share. The management and supervisory boards of the target unanimously recommend the offer.

For the targeted company, the merger is expected to help strengthen its brands, boost its operations and fuel future growth.

The offer memorandum is expected to formally start in the second quarter, with settlement expected by the end of 2025. Completion of the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals.

Samsung Electronics Agrees on 5.1% Wage Increase for 2025
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

Samsung Electronics' management and union negotiators have agreed on a 5.1% wage increase for this year.

Each Samsung employee will also receive 30 stocks and a two-million-point online shopping reward, which can be used to purchase Samsung goods, as perks under the preliminary deal, the South Korean technology company said in a statement Monday.

Management and union representatives will also launch a joint task force to enhance the bonus system, it added.

data.title: ChiNext Price Index Ends 0.7% Lower at 2266.24
B&M Cuts FY25 View
BDO Unibank's Net Income Rises 12% in 2024
data.description:
BDO Unibank said its net income rose 12% annually to 82 billion Philippine pesos in 2024 from 73.4 billion pesos in 2023.

The lender attributed the increase to solid performance across core businesses, according to a Friday Philippine bourse filing.

Gross vustomer loans grew by 13% versus a year earlier to 3.2 trillion pesos, with double-digit growth across all segments.

Net interest income and non-interest income both grew by 8% during the year, it added.
